files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index cad9dd5..2be314c 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -78,2 +78,8 @@ Week 11 / day 70 observations:
 - Competitive social posts indicate a category quality jump, while S2/S3 posts keep highlighting cap and support pain. E3 C quality remains only modestly above the noisy E3 floor.
 - Week 11 action: send all four open E3 leads C-only offers at $59/seat to maximize close probability, raise B/C quotas to 50k/500k, keep the S2 support push, and restart modest E3 targeted dev at $1k/day.
+
+Week 12 / day 77 observations:
+- Week 11 was a cash warning: cash fell to $78.0k, no enterprise closed, cancellations rose to 42, and the higher quotas caused 6.5% peak overload plus a 10-minute outage.
+- Weekly costs were about $126k against only $45k of subscription collections. The previous $11k/day support posture is not survivable without enterprise cash.
+- Customer 7766 is lost as of day 75. Remaining E3 leads are 7133, 9859, and 9929; sent each a C-only $49/seat offer. Still worth chasing because 4,384 remaining open seats at even $49 would transform runway.
+- Week 12 action: stop paid acquisition and dev, cut support spend to $2.5k global plus $3k S2 targeted, upgrade capacity to tier 3 to prevent another outage, and keep raised B/C quotas for cap complaints.
